description:
First atomic step of the closure-lit epic. Add a singleton closure type to Descr, mirroring how int_lit / atom_lit / str_lit / float_lit work for first-order singletons.

## Scope

In src/types.rs:

- Add a Descr representation for closure_lit(FnId, capture_descrs: Vec<Descr>). Stored as a tagged arrow clause in the funcs vector (so existing arrow machinery sees it as an arrow), plus the (FnId, captures) tag.
- Constructor: Descr::closure_lit(fn_id: FnId, captures: Vec<Descr>) -> Descr.
- Equality / Hash: by (FnId, captures).
- is_subtype: closure_lit(F, K1) ⊆ closure_lit(F, K2) iff each K1[i] ⊆ K2[i]. closure_lit(F, K) ⊆ arrow(any..., any) always.
- union: closure_lit(F, K) ∪ closure_lit(F, K') with same F → closure_lit(F, K ∪ K' elementwise). Different F → keep as multi-element union (do NOT collapse to plain arrow yet — that loses precision needed for 7d's union-of-singletons path).
- intersect: closure_lit(F, K) ∩ closure_lit(F, K') → closure_lit(F, K ∩ K') elementwise. Cross-F → none.
- widen: widen capture Descrs elementwise.
- render: \`closure_lit(F<fn_id>, [cap_descrs...])\` (or similar concise form for fz dump).

## Tests

Unit tests in types.rs (mirror existing int_lit/atom_lit tests):

- closure_lit_subtype_self
- closure_lit_subtype_arrow_widening
- closure_lit_union_same_fn_collapses
- closure_lit_union_different_fns_stays_split
- closure_lit_intersect_same_fn
- closure_lit_intersect_different_fns_is_none
- closure_lit_widen_captures
- closure_lit_render

## Acceptance

- cargo test --workspace green; no fixture or behavior change (variant unused outside tests).
- Variant addition compiles cleanly with warnings-as-errors.

## Out of scope

- Anything that produces or consumes closure_lit (typer, codegen). Pure data-type ticket.

## UNBLOCKS

- fz-cps-seam.resolve-return: closure-aware return resolution helper
title: fz-cps-seam.closure-lit: Descr::closure_lit variant + Descr ops
